title: fix: make D1 Tinybird sync atomic
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## What changed

- Pages each D1 table through bounded 5,000-row Worker reads, avoiding the CPU/resource limit that broke the daily sync.
- Builds one NDJSON snapshot per datasource and atomically replaces Tinybird with `mode=replace`.
- Polls and validates the Tinybird job without printing response bodies that may contain private row data.
- Uses a dedicated hashed `D1_EXPORT_TOKEN` for the Worker endpoint; the Tinybird credential remains only in GitHub Actions.
- Fails closed on empty exports and handles cancelling jobs.

## Validation

- `npx vitest run test/admin-auth.test.ts test/d1-tinybird-sync.test.ts` (11 tests)
- `npm run typecheck`
- Biome, Actionlint, Bash syntax, generated bindings, and `git diff --check`

## Rollout

Deploy this Enter revision to the `production` branch before running the updated sync workflow. After deployment, delete the existing `TINYBIRD_SYNC_TOKEN` secret from the Cloudflare Worker; it remains required as a GitHub Actions secret.

gist.headline: Feeding the Tinybird
gist.blurb: Our internal data pipelines just got a major stability upgrade. We're now feeding data to our analytics engine in careful, bite-sized chunks to keep the servers humming happily.
gist.summary: Refactored the daily D1 to Tinybird sync to use 5,000-row paginated Worker reads and atomic replacements, resolving CPU limit timeouts.
gist.impact: Internal analytics pipelines are now reliable and secure, utilizing dedicated hashed export tokens and preventing private row data from appearing in logs.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee carefully feeding glowing data-seeds to a literal tiny pixel bird, representing the paginated, atomic data sync. The Monitor Robot tracks the progress.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, carefully feeding glowing data-seeds to a cute tiny pixelated bird perched on a retro server rack. A chunky monitor robot stands nearby holding a clipboard with a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic, Stardew Valley vibes.
